ucs.title: [LLM] Tonto Team 'Royal Road' RTF weaponizer: EQNEDT32.EXE exploit chain dropping Bisonal/Dexbia
ucs.description: Hunts Tonto Team's signature initial-access chain where a spearphishing RTF built with the shared 'Royal Road / 8.t' weaponizer triggers Microsoft Equation Editor (EQNEDT32.EXE) via CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0798 to drop the Bisonal backdoor or follow-on loaders. EQNEDT32 spawning any child process, or writing PE/script content under %TEMP%/%APPDATA%, is high-fidelity for Royal-Road–class actors and Tonto in particular.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_name) as child_proc values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="eqnedt32.exe" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval suspicious=if(match(child_proc,"(?i)(cmd\.exe|powershell\.exe|wscript\.exe|cscript\.exe|mshta\.exe|rundll32\.exe|regsvr32\.exe|certutil\.exe|bitsadmin\.exe|curl\.exe)"),1,0) | where suspicious=1 | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="eqnedt32.exe" AND (Filesystem.file_name="8.t" OR Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dat")) by Filesystem.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe"]);
let eqnHits = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "eqnedt32.exe"
  | where FileName in~ (suspChildren)
     or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","\\AppData\\","\\Temp\\","FromBase64String","-enc","-w hidden")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessParentFileName;
let eqnDrops = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "eqnedt32.exe"
  | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".dat" or FileName =~ "8.t"
     or FolderPath has_any ("\\Temp\\","\\AppData\\Roaming\\","\\ProgramData\\")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName;
eqnHits
| join kind=leftouter (eqnDrops) on DeviceId
| extend RoyalRoadIndicator = iff(FileName1 =~ "8.t" or FolderPath has "\\Microsoft\\Word\\STARTUP", "high", "medium")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProcess=FileName, ProcessCommandLine, DroppedFile=FileName1, DropPath=FolderPath, SHA256, RoyalRoadIndicator
ucs.rationale: EQNEDT32.EXE has been deprecated/unsupported since 2018 and has no legitimate child-process pattern; Tonto Team (CactusPete/Karma Panda) is one of the small set of Chinese clusters (alongside TA428, Goblin Panda) that continued operationalizing the Royal Road / '8.t' RTF weaponizer well past patching, using it as the primary delivery vector for Bisonal — their hallmark backdoor. Joining EQNEDT32 c

ucs.title: [LLM] Tonto Team Bisonal DLL search-order hijack via legitimate signed loader
ucs.description: Hunts Tonto Team's persistence/execution pattern where Bisonal (and ShadowPad variants they have adopted) is loaded via DLL side-loading from a renamed legitimate signed binary that imports a hijacked DLL co-located in the same writable directory (Temp, AppData, ProgramData). Combines T1574.001 with T1105/T1090.002 beaconing to detect the loader at run-time.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.process_name) as proc values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Users\\*\\AppData\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*")) AND Processes.process_name IN ("chrome_frame_helper.exe","goopdate.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","vmnat.exe","hkcmd.exe","NvSmart.exe","sllauncher.exe","qq.exe","360se.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as side_loaded_dll from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("libcef.dll","goopdate.dll","libexpat.dll","vmnat.dll","version.dll","log.dll") AND Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as c2_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (443,8080,53) AND NOT (All_Traffic.dest_ip IN ("10.0.0.0/8","192.168.0.0/16","172.16.0.0/12")) by All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let sideloadHosts = dynamic(["chrome_frame_helper.exe","goopdate.exe","googleupdate.exe","vmnat.exe","hkcmd.exe","nvsmart.exe","sllauncher.exe","qq.exe","360se.exe"]);
let sideloadDlls = dynamic(["libcef.dll","goopdate.dll","libexpat.dll","vmnat.dll","version.dll","log.dll","secur32.dll"]);
let writableDirs = dynamic(["\\AppData\\","\\Windows\\Temp\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\PerfLogs\\","\\Users\\Public\\"]);
let candidates = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where FileName in~ (sideloadHosts)
  | where FolderPath has_any (writableDirs)
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessId=ProcessId, SHA256, ProcessCommandLine;
let loadedDll = DeviceImageLoadEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where FileName in~ (sideloadDlls)
  | where FolderPath has_any (writableDirs)
  | project DllTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName, DllName=FileName, DllPath=FolderPath, DllSha256=SHA256;
let beacons = DeviceNetworkEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where RemotePort in (443, 8080, 53)
  | where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
  | summarize beaconCount=count(), c2=make_set(RemoteIP, 25) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName;
candidates
| join kind=inner (loadedDll) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| join kind=leftouter (beacons) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, LoaderExe=FileName, LoaderPath=FolderPath, LoaderSha256=SHA256, HijackedDll=DllName, DllPath, DllSha256, ProcessCommandLine, beaconCount, c2
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Tonto Team (BRONZE HUNTLEY / Earth Akhlut) is repeatedly documented by Trend Micro, ESET and SecureWorks staging Bisonal and PlugX/ShadowPad loaders by abusing legitimate signed binaries (Google Update components, VMware vmnat, security-vendor stubs including 360 Total Security and QQ binaries — fitting their Chinese-language tooling preference and East Asian targeting). Detecting a known-good sig
